mads.abstract:
The spelling of the Hiragana to 千代 wasn’t always Chiyo ちよ. The mentions Tokiwa Daijō 常盤大定 in the vols. 8-14. Tokiwa is first mentioned in vol. 8 from 1928. Daijō was not mentioned in the vol. 15 from 1948 anymore, since he died in 1945. As of now, we don’t know until when Chiyo was alive.
In the volumes 8-10 (1928-1934), the name of his wife was written as Chiyoe ちよゑ:
vol. 8 (1928): p. to ト 32 1087/1794
vol. 9 (1931): p. to ト 29 1071/1755
vol. 10 (1934): p. to ト 33 189/902
In the volumes 11-14 (1939-1943) however, the spelling was changed (corrected?) to Chiyo ちよ, which would be the usual Japanese reading for 千代.
vol. 11 (1939): p. to ト 42-43 308/1288
vol. 12 (1940): p. to ト 34 347/1112
vol. 13 (1941): p. to ト 31 238/1000
vol. 14 (1943): p. to ト 29 233/1019
